events.summary: South Korea will soon no longer be one of the few countries where Google Maps doesn't work properly, after its security-conscious government reversed a two-decade stance to approve ‌the export of high-precision map data to overseas servers.  Those conditions include blurring military and other sensitive security-related facilities, as well as ​restricting longitude and latitude coordinates for South Korean territory on products such as Google Maps and Google Earth, it said.  The decision is expected to hurt Naver and Kakao - local internet giants which currently dominate the country's market for digital map services.
events.title: South Korea set to finally a get fully functioning Google Maps

events.summary: Corning's stock has more than doubled due to a significant supply deal with Meta Platforms for fiber-optic cables, essential for AI data centers. The company’s growth is currently limited by its capacity to produce fiber, rather than demand.

events.summary: Linde plc (LIN) is highlighted as a promising low-risk, high-growth stock due to its mature growth profile in clean hydrogen and semiconductor gas sectors. The company boasts a record $10 billion project backlog, primarily locked in by long-term, take-or-pay contracts, ensuring earnings certainty. Its leadership in the energy transition, particularly in clean hydrogen and carbon capture, along with its critical role in supplying ultra-high-purity gases for AI chip manufacturing, positions it for continued growth and makes it an attractive investment, although the article suggests other AI stocks might offer quicker, higher returns.
